metadata.name: FairSlice Platform Economics Ontology
metadata.description: Platform economics ontology governing dynamic equity, revenue sharing, and contribution-based value distribution across PF-Core instances. 9 owned entities: Pie, PieMember, Slice, Claim, WaterfallRule, RevenueEvent, Distribution, SmartContract, License. Automates the 'Fair Slice' principle — equitable, transparent, auditable distribution of value across builders, rainmakers, architects, and partners. Revenue Waterfall engine with 7 priority steps. AI Judge integration for claim verification. Smart Contract Registry extends Unified Registry with licensable business logic. 13 internal relationships + 6 cross-ontology bridges to RRR (roles), PE (processes), GRC-FW (audit), EMC (instances), VSOM (strategy trace), and PARTNER-ONT (channel economics).
metadata.purpose: Provide the definitive model for dynamic equity tracking, contribution-based slice allocation, revenue waterfall distribution, smart contract governance, and AI-verified claim processing. Serves as the economic foundation for PFC-PFI-Product-Client graph cascade — every tier can have its own Pie with inherited rules.
metadata.creator: Azlan EA-AAA

oaa:sourceFrameworks.name: Slicing Pie — Grunt Fund Dynamic Equity Model
Smart Contract Governance (Adapted for AI-Native Platforms)

oaa:imports.name: PARTNER-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Partner, Agency, and Affiliate entities for channel economics

oaa:imports.name: RRR-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Roles, Responsibilities, and Relationships for member role alignment

oaa:imports.name: GRC-FW-ONT
oaa:imports.description: GRC governance hub for audit trail and compliance governance

oaa:imports.name: EMC-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Enterprise Model Composition for instance configuration mapping

oaa:imports.name: PE-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Process Engineering for claim-as-process verification pattern

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Pie Member to RRR Role Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Maps FairSlice pie members to their RRR functional roles — Builder=Engineer, Rainmaker=Sales, Architect=ModuleCreator, Partner=Agency/Affiliate
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Determine slice multiplier based on RRR role classification. Builder roles get 2x, Rainmaker roles get commission-weighted slices.

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Claim to PE Process Verification
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Claims follow a governed process from submission through AI Judge verification to approval/rejection
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Track claim lifecycle through process engineering steps: Submit → Validate → Judge → Approve/Reject → Distribute

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Distribution to GRC Audit Trail
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Every distribution creates an immutable audit entry governed by GRC framework
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Board-level visibility from GRC governance context into distribution audit trail. Ensures every payout is traceable and auditable.

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Smart Contract to Unified Registry
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Smart contracts are registry artifacts following the quasi-OO cascade (Core → Instance → Product → Client)
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Architects publish smart contracts to the Unified Registry. Tenants subscribe via one-click install. Registry cascade enables Core rules inherited by Instances.

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Waterfall to VSOM Strategy Trace
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Waterfall rules trace to strategic intent — every distribution rule has a 'why' anchored in VSOM
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Verify that revenue distribution rules align to strategic objectives. E.g., partner payouts align to S4 (Instance Customisation) growth strategy.

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Partner Referral to Waterfall Payout
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Partner referrals trigger waterfall steps — affiliate payouts and agency retainers
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: When a revenue event occurs, the system checks for active partner referrals and triggers the appropriate waterfall steps for affiliate and agency payouts.

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Pie to EMC Instance Configuration
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Each pie maps to an EMC instance configuration — the pie inherits the instance's ontology set and composition rules
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Determine which ontologies govern a pie's business rules. A BAIV pie inherits BAIV's 7 declared ontologies; a W4M pie inherits W4M's set.

entities.name: Pie
entities.description: A venture, startup, or project whose value is being shared among contributors. The Pie is the top-level tenant in FairSlice — it contains members, slices, waterfall rules, and active smart contracts. Each Pie has a JSONB ontology_config that defines its business rules (multipliers, waterfall priorities, vesting schedules). Maps to the 'pies' table in the Supabase schema. Inherits from PFI instance configuration via EMC.

properties.name: pieId
properties.description: Unique identifier for the pie (UUID)

properties.name: pieName
properties.description: Human-readable name for the venture/project

properties.name: description
properties.description: Description of the venture, its market, and value proposition

properties.name: status
properties.description: Pie lifecycle status: Active, Paused, Dissolved, Graduated

properties.name: ontologyConfig
properties.description: JSONB configuration defining business rules — multipliers per role, waterfall priorities, vesting schedules, custom validation rules. Follows Unified Registry cascade pattern (Core defaults → Instance override → Pie-specific).

properties.name: foundedDate
properties.description: Date the pie was created

properties.name: currency
properties.description: Primary currency for revenue and distributions (ISO 4217 code)

properties.name: totalSlices
properties.description: Calculated field: SUM of all allocated slices. Used to determine ownership percentages.

properties.name: instanceRef
properties.description: Reference to the PFI instance this pie belongs to (e.g., PFI-BAIV, PFI-W4M)

properties.name: stripeAccountId
properties.description: Stripe Connect account ID for payment processing

entities.name: PieMember
entities.description: A participant in a pie — a person or entity that holds slices and receives distributions. Each member has a role (Builder, Rainmaker, Architect, Partner) which determines their slice multiplier and distribution rules. Maps to 'pie_members' join table in Supabase.

properties.name: memberId
properties.description: Unique identifier for the membership record

properties.name: profileRef
properties.description: Reference to the user profile (auth.uid in Supabase)

properties.name: pieRef
properties.description: Reference to the pie this membership belongs to

properties.name: role
properties.description: Member role in this pie: Builder, Rainmaker, Architect, Partner, Advisor, Observer

properties.name: multiplier
properties.description: Slice multiplier for this member's role (e.g., Builder=2.0, Rainmaker=1.0, Architect=1.5)

properties.name: joinedDate
properties.description: Date the member joined the pie

properties.name: status
properties.description: Membership status: Active, Inactive, Vested-Out, Departed

properties.name: vestingSchedule
properties.description: Optional vesting schedule: cliff period, vesting duration, acceleration triggers

properties.name: sliceCount
properties.description: Calculated field: total slices held by this member in this pie

properties.name: ownershipPercentage
properties.description: Calculated field: (memberSlices / totalPieSlices) * 100

entities.name: Slice
entities.description: A unit of contribution to a pie. Slices are the base currency of FairSlice — they represent time, money, IP, or other contributions. The slice's value is determined by its type and the member's multiplier. A Builder contributing 10 hours at 2x multiplier earns 20 slices. Total ownership = member's slices / total pie slices.

properties.name: sliceId
properties.description: Unique identifier for this slice allocation

properties.name: memberRef
properties.description: Reference to the PieMember who earned these slices

properties.name: sliceType
properties.description: Type of contribution: Equity, Time, Cash, IP, Sweat-Equity

properties.name: baseUnits
properties.description: Raw contribution units before multiplier (hours, dollars, deliverables)

properties.name: multiplier
properties.description: Multiplier applied at allocation time (from member's role multiplier)

properties.name: totalSlices
properties.description: Calculated: baseUnits * multiplier. The actual slice count added.

properties.name: claimRef
properties.description: Reference to the approved Claim that generated these slices (null for founder allocations)

properties.name: allocatedDate
properties.description: Date and time the slices were allocated

properties.name: description
properties.description: Human-readable description of the contribution

entities.name: Claim
entities.description: A contribution claim submitted by a pie member for verification. Claims are the input to the AI Judge — they contain proof of contribution and the requested slice allocation. The AI Judge reads the pie's active smart contracts to verify the claim, then approves/rejects. Approved claims generate Slice allocations.

properties.name: claimId
properties.description: Unique identifier for this claim

properties.name: claimantRef
properties.description: Reference to the PieMember submitting the claim

properties.name: pieRef
properties.description: Reference to the pie this claim is for

properties.name: claimType
properties.description: Type of contribution being claimed: CodeShipped, DealClosed, ModuleCreated, ContentDelivered, CashInvested, TimeContributed

properties.name: proof
properties.description: Evidence of contribution: git commit hashes, invoice references, CRM deal IDs, deliverable URLs, time logs

properties.name: requestedSlices
properties.description: Number of base slices requested (before multiplier)

properties.name: status
properties.description: Claim lifecycle: Pending, Under-Review, Approved, Rejected, Disputed

properties.name: submittedDate
properties.description: Date and time the claim was submitted

properties.name: resolvedDate
properties.description: Date and time the claim was approved/rejected

properties.name: judgeReasoning
properties.description: The AI Judge's reasoning for the decision — stored verbatim for audit

properties.name: smartContractRef
properties.description: Reference to the SmartContract used to evaluate this claim

entities.name: WaterfallRule
entities.description: A priority step in the revenue distribution waterfall. When a RevenueEvent arrives, the waterfall rules execute in priority order: Platform Fee first, then Affiliate Payout, Agency Retainer, Architect Royalty, OpEx Recovery, Tax Reserve, and finally Dividend Distribution. Each rule defines what percentage or fixed amount is extracted at that priority level.

properties.name: ruleId
properties.description: Unique identifier for this waterfall rule

properties.name: pieRef
properties.description: Reference to the pie this rule belongs to

properties.name: priority
properties.description: Execution priority (1 = first, higher = later). Must be unique within a pie.

properties.name: stepType
properties.description: Waterfall step type: Platform-Fee, Affiliate-Payout, Agency-Retainer, Architect-Royalty, OpEx-Recovery, Tax-Reserve, Dividend-Distribution

properties.name: calculationType
properties.description: How to calculate: Percentage, Fixed-Amount, Remainder

properties.name: value
properties.description: The percentage (0-100) or fixed amount for this step

properties.name: recipientType
properties.description: Who receives: Platform, Affiliate, Agency, Architect, Member, Pool, TaxAuthority

properties.name: recipientRef
properties.description: Specific recipient reference (partner ID, member ID, or 'pool' for dividend)

properties.name: conditions
properties.description: Optional conditions for this step (e.g., only applies if cash > threshold, only if partner referral exists)

properties.name: status
properties.description: Rule status: Active, Paused, Expired

entities.name: RevenueEvent
entities.description: An incoming revenue or transaction event for a pie. Revenue events trigger the waterfall — the system processes each WaterfallRule in priority order, creating Distribution records for each step. Sources include: SaaS subscriptions, one-time sales, marketplace licenses, consulting fees, and external payments.

properties.name: eventId
properties.description: Unique identifier for this revenue event

properties.name: pieRef
properties.description: Reference to the pie receiving the revenue

properties.name: amount
properties.description: Gross revenue amount before waterfall distribution

properties.name: currency
properties.description: Currency code (ISO 4217)

properties.name: source
properties.description: Revenue source: SaaS-Subscription, One-Time-Sale, Marketplace-License, Consulting-Fee, External-Payment

properties.name: sourceRef
properties.description: External reference: Stripe payment ID, invoice number, contract reference

properties.name: eventDate
properties.description: Date and time the revenue was received

properties.name: processedDate
properties.description: Date and time the waterfall was processed

properties.name: status
properties.description: Event status: Pending, Processing, Distributed, Failed, Reversed

properties.name: netToPie
properties.description: Calculated: amount remaining after all waterfall deductions

entities.name: Distribution
entities.description: A calculated payout from a revenue event. Each Distribution represents one step in the waterfall execution — it records exactly how much went to whom, via which rule, from which event. Distributions are immutable once created (audit requirement BR-FS-004).

properties.name: distributionId
properties.description: Unique identifier for this distribution

properties.name: eventRef
properties.description: Reference to the RevenueEvent that triggered this distribution

properties.name: waterfallRuleRef
properties.description: Reference to the WaterfallRule applied

properties.name: recipientRef
properties.description: Reference to the recipient (PieMember ID, Partner ID, or 'platform')

properties.name: amount
properties.description: Amount distributed in this step

properties.name: distributionType
properties.description: Type: Cash-Payout, Equity-Grant, Royalty-Payment, Commission-Payment, Platform-Fee, Tax-Withholding

properties.name: distributedDate
properties.description: Date and time the distribution was calculated

properties.name: paymentStatus
properties.description: Payment status: Calculated, Queued, Paid, Failed

properties.name: stripeTransferId
properties.description: Stripe transfer ID if paid via Stripe Connect

entities.name: SmartContract
entities.description: A packaged set of validation rules and business logic that governs specific operations within a pie. Smart Contracts are published by Architects to the Unified Registry and can be licensed by any pie. They define the system prompt and validation rules for the AI Judge. Examples: contribution verification rules, commission triggers, vesting schedules, royalty calculations, compliance checks.

properties.name: contractId
properties.description: Unique identifier for this smart contract

properties.name: name
properties.description: Human-readable name for the contract

properties.name: version
properties.description: Semantic version (contracts are immutable; new versions for changes)

properties.name: category
properties.description: Contract category: Contribution-Verification, Commission-Trigger, Vesting-Schedule, Royalty-Calculation, Compliance-Check, Custom

properties.name: authorRef
properties.description: Reference to the Architect (PieMember) who created this contract

properties.name: systemPrompt
properties.description: The system prompt template used by the AI Judge when evaluating claims against this contract. Supports variable interpolation for pie-specific context.

properties.name: validationRules
properties.description: Structured validation rules: conditions, thresholds, required evidence types, scoring criteria

properties.name: licensingTerms
properties.description: How this contract can be used: {feeType: 'cash'|'equity'|'free', amount: N, currency: 'GBP', billingCycle: 'monthly'|'one-time'}

properties.name: status
properties.description: Contract status: Draft, Published, Deprecated, Archived

properties.name: registryArtifactRef
properties.description: Reference to the Unified Registry artifact entry (pfc:RegistryArtifact)

properties.name: publishedDate
properties.description: Date the contract was published to the registry

properties.name: installCount
properties.description: Number of pies currently using this contract

entities.name: License
entities.description: A subscription linking a SmartContract to a specific Pie. When a tenant installs a smart contract via one-click install, a License record is created. The license defines the terms (cash fee, equity fee, or free), duration, and activation status. Royalty payments to the Architect are triggered via the waterfall.

properties.name: licenseId
properties.description: Unique identifier for this license

properties.name: contractRef
properties.description: Reference to the SmartContract being licensed

properties.name: pieRef
properties.description: Reference to the pie using this contract

properties.name: feeType
properties.description: Payment type: Cash, Equity, Free

properties.name: feeAmount
properties.description: Fee amount per billing cycle (null for free licenses)

properties.name: billingCycle
properties.description: Billing frequency: Monthly, Quarterly, Annual, One-Time

properties.name: activatedDate
properties.description: Date the license was activated

properties.name: expiresDate
properties.description: License expiration date (null for perpetual)

properties.name: status
properties.description: License status: Active, Suspended, Expired, Cancelled

relationships.name: Pie Has Member
relationships.description: Links a Pie to its participating members

relationships.name: Member Holds Slices
relationships.description: Links a PieMember to their allocated slices

relationships.name: Claim Requests Slice
relationships.description: Links an approved Claim to the Slice allocation it generated

relationships.name: Claim Submitted By
relationships.description: Links a Claim to the PieMember who submitted it

relationships.name: Pie Has Waterfall Rule
relationships.description: Links a Pie to its revenue waterfall rules (ordered by priority)

relationships.name: Revenue Event For Pie
relationships.description: Links a RevenueEvent to the Pie receiving the revenue

relationships.name: Distribution From Event
relationships.description: Links a Distribution to the RevenueEvent that triggered it

relationships.name: Distribution To Recipient
relationships.description: Links a Distribution to its recipient (PieMember or Partner)

relationships.name: Distribution Via Rule
relationships.description: Links a Distribution to the WaterfallRule that calculated it

relationships.name: Contract Verifies Claim
relationships.description: Links a SmartContract to the Claims it has been used to evaluate

relationships.name: License For Contract
relationships.description: Links a License to the SmartContract it grants access to

relationships.name: License In Pie
relationships.description: Links a License to the Pie that subscribed to it

relationships.name: Contract Published By
relationships.description: Links a SmartContract to the Architect who created and published it

relationships.name: Member Maps To RRR Role
relationships.description: Maps a FairSlice PieMember to their corresponding RRR functional role. Builder=Engineer, Rainmaker=Sales, Architect=ModuleCreator, Partner=Agency/Affiliate.

relationships.name: Claim Follows Process
relationships.description: Claims follow a governed PE process: Submit → Validate → Judge → Approve/Reject → Distribute

relationships.name: Distribution Audited By
relationships.description: Every distribution is audited under GRC governance — immutable audit trail with input, reasoning, and output

relationships.name: Pie Maps To Instance
relationships.description: Maps a Pie to its governing EMC InstanceConfiguration — the pie inherits the instance's ontology set and composition rules

relationships.name: Waterfall Traces Strategy
relationships.description: Every waterfall rule traces to a VSOM strategy — the 'why' behind every distribution rule

relationships.name: Contract In Registry
relationships.description: Smart contracts are registered as artifacts in the Unified Registry, following the quasi-OO cascade

businessRules.name: Slice Ownership Calculation
businessRules.condition: IF slices are allocated to a PieMember
businessRules.action: THEN ownershipPercentage MUST equal (memberSlices / SUM(allPieSlices)) * 100 AND totalSlices on Pie MUST be updated

businessRules.name: Waterfall Priority Uniqueness
businessRules.condition: IF WaterfallRules exist for a Pie
businessRules.action: THEN each rule MUST have a unique priority value AND Platform-Fee MUST be priority 1

businessRules.name: Claim Requires Proof
businessRules.action: THEN proof MUST contain at least one evidence item (commit hash, invoice ref, contract ref, URL, or time log)

businessRules.name: Distribution Audit Trail
businessRules.action: THEN an immutable audit log entry MUST be created with exact input data, AI Judge reasoning (if applicable), and output amounts

businessRules.name: Immutable Attribution
businessRules.condition: IF a partner referral attribution is attached to a Pie
businessRules.action: THEN the referral code and partner link MUST be immutable — cannot be changed, reassigned, or deleted (prevents commission hijacking)

businessRules.name: Smart Contract Versioning
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have a semantic version AND active contracts MUST NOT be modified in-place — new versions MUST be created for changes

businessRules.name: Multiplier Transparency
businessRules.condition: IF a slice multiplier is applied to a member
businessRules.action: THEN the multiplier MUST be declared in the Pie's ontologyConfig before being used AND visible to all pie members

businessRules.name: Waterfall Sum Constraint
businessRules.condition: IF WaterfallRules are configured for a Pie
businessRules.action: THEN the sum of all percentage-based rules MUST NOT exceed 100% AND the final step SHOULD be Dividend-Distribution with calculationType=Remainder

businessRules.name: AI Judge Reasoning Persistence
businessRules.condition: IF the AI Judge processes a Claim
businessRules.action: THEN the exact system prompt, input data, and output reasoning MUST be stored in audit_logs AND the Claim's judgeReasoning field MUST be populated

businessRules.name: Tenant Isolation
businessRules.condition: IF a user accesses Pie data
businessRules.action: THEN RLS policies MUST enforce that only pie_members of that pie (or authorised agency partners) can read/write data

businessRules.name: Revenue Event Completeness
businessRules.action: THEN netToPie MUST equal (amount - SUM(all distributions)) AND the event status MUST transition to Distributed

businessRules.name: License Required for Contract Use
businessRules.condition: IF a SmartContract is used to evaluate a Claim in a Pie
businessRules.action: THEN an active License MUST exist linking that contract to that pie

hasDefinedTerm.name: Active
hasDefinedTerm.description: Pie is operational, accepting contributions and distributing revenue

hasDefinedTerm.name: Paused
hasDefinedTerm.description: Pie operations temporarily suspended — no new claims or distributions

hasDefinedTerm.name: Dissolved
hasDefinedTerm.description: Pie has been formally dissolved — final distributions calculated

hasDefinedTerm.name: Graduated
hasDefinedTerm.description: Pie has transitioned to a formal entity (Ltd, LLC) — FairSlice governance replaced by corporate structure

oaa:enumerations.name: MemberRole
oaa:enumerations.description: Roles a PieMember can hold. Maps to RRR functional roles.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Builder
hasDefinedTerm.description: Engineer/developer — earns 2x multiplier for code shipped. Maps to rrr:Engineer.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Rainmaker
hasDefinedTerm.description: Sales/business development — earns commission + equity. Maps to rrr:Sales.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Architect
hasDefinedTerm.description: Module/smart contract creator — earns royalties. Maps to rrr:ModuleCreator.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Partner
hasDefinedTerm.description: Agency or affiliate — earns override/revenue share. Maps to partner:Partner.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Advisor
hasDefinedTerm.description: Strategic advisor — earns equity for guidance. Typically lower multiplier.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Observer
hasDefinedTerm.description: Read-only access — investors, board members. No slices earned.

oaa:enumerations.name: SliceType
oaa:enumerations.description: Types of contribution that earn slices.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Equity
hasDefinedTerm.description: Direct equity allocation (founder shares, investor stakes)

hasDefinedTerm.name: Time
hasDefinedTerm.description: Time-based contribution (hours worked, sprints completed)

hasDefinedTerm.name: Cash
hasDefinedTerm.description: Cash investment (seed funding, operating expenses paid)

hasDefinedTerm.name: IP
hasDefinedTerm.description: Intellectual property contribution (patents, designs, smart contracts)

hasDefinedTerm.name: Sweat-Equity
hasDefinedTerm.description: Non-cash contribution of labour or expertise before revenue

hasDefinedTerm.name: Pending
hasDefinedTerm.description: Claim submitted, awaiting review

hasDefinedTerm.name: Under-Review
hasDefinedTerm.description: AI Judge is evaluating the claim against active smart contracts

hasDefinedTerm.name: Approved
hasDefinedTerm.description: Claim verified and slices allocated

hasDefinedTerm.name: Rejected
hasDefinedTerm.description: Claim failed verification — reasoning stored in judgeReasoning

hasDefinedTerm.name: Disputed
hasDefinedTerm.description: Claim decision challenged by claimant — escalated for human review

oaa:enumerations.name: WaterfallStepType
oaa:enumerations.description: Types of waterfall distribution steps, in typical priority order.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Platform-Fee
hasDefinedTerm.description: FairSlice SaaS platform fee — always priority 1

hasDefinedTerm.name: Affiliate-Payout
hasDefinedTerm.description: Auto-redirect X% of platform fee to referring affiliate

hasDefinedTerm.name: Agency-Retainer
hasDefinedTerm.description: Management fee paid to agency managing this pie

hasDefinedTerm.name: Architect-Royalty
hasDefinedTerm.description: License fee for smart contracts used by this pie

hasDefinedTerm.name: OpEx-Recovery
hasDefinedTerm.description: Repayment of operating expenses and cash contributions

hasDefinedTerm.name: Tax-Reserve
hasDefinedTerm.description: Withholding for tax obligations

hasDefinedTerm.name: Dividend-Distribution
hasDefinedTerm.description: Remaining profit distributed to shareholders by ownership %

oaa:enumerations.name: ContractCategory
oaa:enumerations.description: Categories of smart contracts in the registry.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Contribution-Verification
hasDefinedTerm.description: Validates contribution claims (code shipped, deals closed, content delivered)

hasDefinedTerm.name: Commission-Trigger
hasDefinedTerm.description: Triggers commission payouts based on revenue events or deal closure

hasDefinedTerm.name: Vesting-Schedule
hasDefinedTerm.description: Defines equity vesting rules — cliff, duration, acceleration triggers

hasDefinedTerm.name: Royalty-Calculation
hasDefinedTerm.description: Calculates royalty payments for licensed smart contracts or IP

hasDefinedTerm.name: Compliance-Check
hasDefinedTerm.description: Validates regulatory or governance compliance requirements

hasDefinedTerm.name: Custom
hasDefinedTerm.description: Custom business logic defined by the pie's ontology_config

hasDefinedTerm.name: Cash-Payout
hasDefinedTerm.description: Cash payment to a member or partner

hasDefinedTerm.name: Equity-Grant
hasDefinedTerm.description: Equity allocation (slices) rather than cash

hasDefinedTerm.name: Royalty-Payment
hasDefinedTerm.description: Royalty to a smart contract architect

hasDefinedTerm.name: Commission-Payment
hasDefinedTerm.description: Commission to a sales member or partner

hasDefinedTerm.name: Platform-Fee
hasDefinedTerm.description: Fee paid to the FairSlice platform

hasDefinedTerm.name: Tax-Withholding
hasDefinedTerm.description: Tax reserve withholding

hasDefinedTerm.name: CodeShipped
hasDefinedTerm.description: Code merged to main branch — proof: git commit hashes, PR references

hasDefinedTerm.name: DealClosed
hasDefinedTerm.description: Sales deal closed — proof: CRM deal ID, signed contract, invoice

hasDefinedTerm.name: ModuleCreated
hasDefinedTerm.description: Smart contract or module published — proof: registry artifact ID, version

hasDefinedTerm.name: ContentDelivered
hasDefinedTerm.description: Content or deliverable completed — proof: URL, client sign-off

hasDefinedTerm.name: CashInvested
hasDefinedTerm.description: Cash investment made — proof: bank transfer ref, invoice

hasDefinedTerm.name: TimeContributed
hasDefinedTerm.description: Time-based contribution — proof: time log, sprint report, deliverable

bridges.description: PieMember roles align to RRR functional roles — Builder=Engineer, Rainmaker=Sales, Architect=ModuleCreator
Claims follow a governed PE process lifecycle: Submit → Validate → Judge → Approve/Reject → Distribute
Every distribution is audited under GRC governance with immutable audit trail
Pies inherit PFI instance configuration — ontology set and composition rules
Waterfall rules trace to VSOM strategies — every distribution has strategic justification
Partner referrals trigger waterfall steps for affiliate payouts and agency retainers

crossOntologyReferences.description: FAIRSLICE-ONT bridges to RRR (role alignment), PE (process governance), GRC-FW (audit trail), EMC (instance configuration), VSOM (strategy trace), and PARTNER (channel economics). It sits in the Orchestration series alongside EMC-ONT, governing the economic layer of the PFC platform.

changeControl.changeNotes: Initial release: 9 owned entities (Pie, PieMember, Slice, Claim, WaterfallRule, RevenueEvent, Distribution, SmartContract, License). 19 relationships (13 internal + 6 cross-ontology). 12 business rules. 8 enumerations. 7 join patterns. Revenue Waterfall engine with 7 priority steps. AI Judge integration pattern. Smart Contract Registry extending Unified Registry. VP-RRR alignment: Problem→Risk (contributor under-reward), Solution→Requirement (transparent ledger), Benefit→Result (equitable returns). Epic 50 F50.1.

versionHistory.notes: Initial release. Platform economics for dynamic equity and revenue sharing. Slicing Pie model adapted for PFC agentic platform. Revenue Waterfall: Platform Fee → Affiliate → Agency → Architect Royalty → OpEx → Tax → Dividend. Smart Contract Registry as Unified Registry artifact type. AI Judge claim verification. 6 cross-ontology bridges. Epic 50 F50.1.

competencyQuestions.question: What percentage of the pie does contributor X currently own?
When $100 of revenue arrives, how is it distributed across the waterfall?
Which smart contracts govern claim verification in pie Y?
What is the complete audit trail for distribution Z?
Which partner referrals are attached to pie Y and what commission do they earn?
